codeReview.summary: The Marcus Aurelius persona system is well-implemented with PersonaService, proper Stoic character definition, and integration into session endpoints. However, there are concerns around duplicate system prompts and missing error handling in database queries.

issues.description: MARCUS_BASE_PROMPT is nearly identical to MARCUS_SYSTEM_PROMPT in LLMService (llm.py:53). This duplication could lead to drift where the two prompts diverge. Consider having PersonaService import and reuse the prompt from LLMService, or consolidating prompt management in one place.

PersonaService.__init__() contains only 'pass' with no actual initialization. While not causing bugs, this is unusual. Either remove the docstring/implementation or initialize service dependencies here if needed in the future.
get_user_profile() performs a database query with no try/except. If the query fails, it will raise an unhandled exception. Consider wrapping in try/except with logging for resilience.
get_recent_assertions() has no error handling for database queries. A query failure would bubble up unhandled.
get_session_count() has no error handling for database queries.

get_emotional_history() uses PsychUpdate joined with SemanticAssertion but the join condition only filters by SemanticAssertion.user_id. This may work but the join semantics are non-obvious - a comment explaining why the join is needed would improve readability.

sharedStateObservations.observation: MARCUS_SYSTEM_PROMPT was already defined in LLMService, but the worker created a new MARCUS_BASE_PROMPT in PersonaService. AGENTS.md mentions 'config/prompts.yaml' for persona prompts but the actual implementation uses hardcoded constants. Neither the duplication nor the divergence from AGENTS.md's suggested file-based approach is documented.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: LLMService.MARCUS_SYSTEM_PROMPT at llm.py:53, PersonaService.MARCUS_BASE_PROMPT at persona.py:46. AGENTS.md line ~'config/prompts.yaml' suggests YAML-based prompt management.

sharedStateObservations.observation: PersonaService was added as a new service but was not documented in .factory/services.yaml. The skill procedure mentions verifying commands but not that new services should be registered in services.yaml.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: backend-python/src/services/persona.py defines PersonaService but no corresponding entry in .factory/services.yaml

sharedStateObservations.observation: The skill references /Users/stefano/repos/stoic-emperor/src/core/emperor_brain.py and /Users/stefano/repos/aigent/docs/memory-architecture.md for patterns, but these paths were not verified as accessible. The worker followed the procedure but spent time discovering the actual memory patterns independently.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: SKILL.md backend-worker: 'Review Stoic Emperor patterns in /Users/stefano/repos/stoic-emperor/src/core/emperor_brain.py' - these paths are external references not in the mission repo

sharedStateObservations.observation: Database query methods (get_user_profile, get_recent_assertions, etc.) in services typically wrap queries in try/except blocks with logging. The PersonaService uses bare queries without this pattern. AGENTS.md does not explicitly specify error handling conventions for services.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Compare persona.py get_user_profile (line 77) with other services like session.py which typically use try/except blocks

summary: The Marcus Aurelius persona feature is implemented correctly with good test coverage (26 tests) and proper integration into both add_message and stream_message endpoints. The Stoic character is well-defined and persona context building incorporates user profile, semantic assertions, and emotional history. Main concerns are duplicate system prompt definitions and lack of error handling in database queries. The implementation fulfills VAL-AI-005 and VAL-AI-006 requirements.
